steps.title: Use the honeybee threshold
steps.content: The passage says foraging honeybees have a maximum positive charge high enough to facilitate electrostatic pollen transfer. The graph must therefore support a pollinator that reaches at least that same useful charge level.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A points to houseflies, which the graph does not show meeting the honeybee charge level. Choice C invents a specific minimum above 100 pC. Choice D misstates the data and says pollinators with greater charges should not show the capacity. Anna's hummingbirds match the needed charge comparison. The answer is B.
